info.name: Dimitri Buhon
about: Software engineer with 10+ years of experience, from early-stage startups to large orgs, on products used by millions of people. I strive to find simple solutions to complex problems.

career.quote: Themis is the first compliance collaboration tool that helps companies accelerate partnerships with vendors, banks, credit unions, and fintechs, and is now used by more than 50 banks, credit unions, and fintechs, serving thousands of users daily.
description: Built and hired the frontend engineering team from scratch, growing it to 6 engineers, and set the technical strategy and architecture for the company, working directly with the founders on roadmap and tradeoffs.
Led the end-to-end rearchitecture of the web app from a monolithic to a modular structure, improving scalability, code quality, and separation of concerns.
Migrated incrementally, module by module, behind route-level boundaries and feature flags, avoiding a feature freeze.
That rearchitecture helped cut CI build times from ~40 to 10 minutes, reclaiming an estimated $150K+ per year in engineering time and CI compute costs previously lost to CI waits and reruns.
Improved page load times from as much as a minute down to ~3 seconds by adding caching and optimistic UI updates.
Built a set of shared libraries (UI, components, queries) used across more than 40 features, improving UI consistency and increasing feature delivery by more than 100%.
Led development of several major features, including a configurable dashboard with widgets and charts and a fully configurable automation system covering notifications, recurring tasks, and more.
Established the frontend engineering standards, coding practices, and code review guidelines the team works from.
Shaped how the team works day to day: moved standups to a quicker, async format, reorganized the team's Linear workflow, and introduced project planning and estimation practices.
Introduced AI-assisted workflows: MCP integrations with Git, Linear, and Slack, plus automated code review.
Built a Slack app used internally that automatically rotates and assigns on-call each week.

career.quote: Arctic Wolf delivers dynamic 24x7 cybersecurity protection tailored to the specific needs of your organization.
description: Led the migration to an Nx monorepo, unifying several frontend dashboards into a single, consistent codebase, adopted across an engineering org of hundreds of engineers at a company that grew to 4,000+ employees serving 10,000+ customer organizations.
Cut CI build times as part of the monorepo migration, meaningfully reducing engineering time lost to CI waits and compute costs across the org.
Wrote an RFC and gave an internal tech talk proposing the monorepo migration to build cross-team buy-in before rollout.
Raised coding standards company-wide with linting, testing, and architecture guidelines adopted across multiple orgs.
Built a shared component library used by every team on the unified dashboard.
Owned frontend architecture on the platform team and led feature delivery across multiple teams (React, Golang), including hands-on help shipping other teams' features.

Mentored my team (~5 engineers) on frontend architecture and testing, onboarded leads from 5 other teams onto the new standards, gave internal tech talks reaching dozens of engineers, and carried on-call.

career.quote: Unity is the world's leading platform for creating and operating interactive, real-time 3D (RT3D) content.
description: Led a full rewrite of the Ads SDK webview, handling millions of requests a day, after the legacy codebase had become too complex to maintain or ship new features on.

Led development of several major features (including a change-history feature) and contributed to others for the Unity Dashboard, used by hundreds of engineers across dozens of teams and millions of end users.
Helped shape platform architecture decisions and pushed teams to adopt better development practices, including TypeScript and automated linting.
Wrote RFCs and presented findings in internal tech talks to evaluate solutions and drive adoption across teams, including for state management, caching, and more.
Maintained the shared frontend component library underpinning the Unity Dashboard and other product surfaces.

career.quote: LVL designs, builds and operates media technology solutions for the global media and entertainment market.
description: Built a configurable Netflix-like web app (React, TypeScript) letting operators customize content, and video/audio playback without writing code.
Built a web framework with TypeScript.

projects.title: Polling Social Network
description: Set up an Nx monorepo with a Next.js frontend, CI, and automated deployment.
Built the backend: Supabase Postgres RPCs, edge functions, row-level security, embedding-based semantic search.
Implemented authentication, including email, Google, Twitter, and phone sign-up.
Designed and built the whole platform, from database schema to UI.
Deployed and running in production.

projects.title: On-call Rotations
description: Built a Slack app that automatically selects and rotates the on-call person each week based on a configurable queue.
Keeps a Slack user group in sync with the current on-call person, so mentions always reach the right person.
Deployed on Railway with a freemium pricing model.
